case_title: BAOLAI TANG VS. LAZ PARKING CALIFORNIA, LLC ET AL

docket_entries.description: COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR (TRANSACTION ID # 62370768) FILED BY PLAINTIFF TANG, BAOLAI DBA GRACE JEWELRY

LAW AND MOTION, THE LAW FIRM OF HEDANI, CHOY, SPALDING & SALVAGIONE LLP'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF BAOLAI TANG IS GRANTED. THE CMC DATE OF NOVEMBER 7, 2018 STILL REMAINS ON CALENDAR PER THE REQUEST OF THE PLAINTIFF, HOWEVER IT MAY REQUIRE A FEW MONTHS FOR MS. TANG TO HIRE AN ATTORNEY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE CITY'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NO OPPOSITION FILED. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)**THIS MATTER WAS CALLED AT 9:30 A.M. AS BEING UNCONTESTED. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT CCSF, SUBMITS AN ORDER THAT IS SIGNED BY THE COURT. AFTER THE MATTER WAS CALLED AND THE ORDER WAS SIGNED, AND DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL HAVE LEFT, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER APPEARS IN COURT AT 9:40 A.M. THE COURT EXPLAINS TO PLAINTIFF THAT THIS MATTER HAS ALREADY BEEN CALLED AND THAT AN ORDER HAS BEEN SIGNED. PLAINTIFF STATES THAT SHE DOES NOT UNDERSTAND ENGLISH. **

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-02-2020 Continued To Mar-08-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Charlie Yu, Esq. 415-778-0800 cyu@hedanichoy.com of Hedani Choy Spalding & Salvagione LLP for Plaintiff.
Stuart Home III Esq. 415-836-2626 stuart.home@aig.com of Skebba Isaac & Buechler for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT LAZ PARKING CALIFORNIA, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And/Or Summary Adjudication.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant Laz Parking California, LLC's motion for summary judgment is granted. Laz Parking does not have a general duty to safeguard plaintiff's property; therefore, plaintiff cannot establish its prima facie case for premises liability or negligence. In the commercial property context, absent an express covenant on the point or the landlord's voluntarily undertaking to protect a tenant's property, landlords do not owe a duty to safeguard tenant property from reasonably foreseeable criminal activity (burglary). (Royal Neckwear Co., Inc. v. Century City, Inc. (1988) 205 Cal.App.3d 1146, 1151; see Rotman v. Maclin Markets, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1709, 1715 [lessor not liable for theft of tenant's jewelry merchandise in market rental space because no agreement to provide such security and no evidence lessor voluntarily assumed such duty].) Plaintiff attempts to argue that Laz Parking has a duty to maintain and operate the garage in a reasonable manner, following certain protocols. (Opposition 4:19-22; 5:6-11; 6:9-13.) However, it is undisputed that plaintiff does not have a contract with defendant Laz (UMF #2) and there are no facts or evidence that suggests Laz Parking assumed a duty to protect plaintiff's property.

Plaintiff's request for a continuance pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Sec 437c(h) is denied. Plaintiff's counsel's declaration does not demonstrate that a continuance is needed in order to obtain facts to oppose this motion, specifically to the issue of duty. (Bahl v. Bank of America (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 389, 395.) Instead, counsel's declaration asserts that further discovery may yield evidence that Laz's employee is somehow responsible for the burglary, thus necessitating plaintiff to add the employee as a DOE defendant in which Laz Parking may be vicariously liable and/or liable for negligent hiring and supervision. (Yu Decl., Paras. 2-4.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
